source_units.text_raw:
The following regulations shall apply to all building permit applications for properties located within the 500 Lot Area, as defined and described in Figure 6.1 and covering the Downtown Zones DC, DMS, DMU, DN and W.
Buildings or districts that have been designated as 'designated heritage resources' are subject to the City of Charlottetown Heritage Preservation Bylaw and shall not be altered without a heritage permit.
RESOURCES Notwithstanding the minimum front yard setback in each downtown zone, if the site borders a designated heritage resource site, the setback shall not be less than the average setback of the neighbouring heritage resource.
The Front and flankage facades shall carry a consistent type and quality of materials. Where a Side Yard Setback is greater than 3 m (9.8 ft), the Side facade shall also carry a consistent material. This clause also applies to the rear facade on a corner lot when the Rear Yard is greater than 3 m (9.8 ft).
Prohibited front or flanking facade materials in the 500 Lot Area include:
Vinyl siding
Cement and aluminum clap-board siding
EIFS / synthetic stucco (Exterior Insulation and Finish Systems)
Imitation stone/brick panels (faux masonry veneer), stone-tile, plastic laminates or other synthetic materials that mimic real stone or real wood.
Concrete block (CMU) exposed finishes
Unfinished or painted metal sheet siding
Reflective or mirrored glass
Plastic or plywood
Notwithstanding Section 6.6.2 above, vinyl siding or cement clap-board may be permitted on the Rear of the building, or the side of the building if the side yard is no less than 3m as per 6.6.1.
Where a prohibited material currently exists it may be maintained and replaced as necessary.
The Front and flankage facades shall carry a consistent style and material of windows. Where a Side Yard Setback is greater than 3 m (9.8 ft), the Side facade shall also carry a consistent style and material of windows. This clause also applies to the rear facade on a corner lot when the Rear Yard is greater than 3 m (9.8 ft).
For Additions to, or for a Renovation to an existing structure with:
Windows with simulated divided lights with a spacer bar, new windows shall also have simulated divided lights with a spacer bar.
Single or double hung windows, new windows shall also be single or double hung.
The expression of the roof shall be clearly distinguished from the rest of the building through treatments such as step-backs, change in materials, cornices lines, and overhangs when architecturally appropriate for the design of the building.
Mechanical penthouses shall be integrated within the architectural treatment of roofs shall be screened from view.
Ducted heat pumps or mini-splits shall not be visible on the front facade of buildings
All disturbed surfaces shall be seeded, sodded or hydroseeded with grass or meadow mixture or restored to natural condition.
A Landscaped Area or hard surfaced plaza shall be provided between the sidewalk and the front facade of the building. ZONING & DEVELOPMENT BYL AW Design Standards for 500 Lot Area |
Front doors shall connect to adjacent sidewalks by a hard surfaced walkway no less than 1.8 m wide
For buildings that have been set back more than 3 m from the front lot line, no less than 1 tree shall be planted per 10 m of frontage.
Chain-link fencing is prohibited for all residential or commercial uses
Existing mature trees (>100 mm caliper) shall be preserved wherever possible. Removals require replacement with a similar caliper tree or a cash in lieu payment .
Where work is undertaken within the dripline or root zone of a public tree, a City arborist shall be consulted.
For multi-unit dwellings that exceed 8 units, the landscaping requirements for any multi-unit dwelling that directly borders any adjoining single detached, semi, duplex or townhouse lots include:
No less than a 2 m wide landscape buffer along the periphery boundary.
The buffer shall include no less than 1 tree per 10 m of length.
A 1.8 m minimum opaque wood or stone fence along the property side or rear yard boundary
If the patio decks of the multi-unit dwelling are within 25 m of a property line, the landscape buffer shall be widened to 3 m and the trees shall be increased to 1 tree per 6 m of boundary length.
The buffer requirements may be increased or decreased by the development officer or heritage officer depending on the context of the adjacency and the scale of the development.
Surface Parking is not permitted in the Front Yard or flankage yard unless:
Attached to a porte cochere or drop off for a hotel;
For single detached units that are setback more than 10 m from the street right-of-way;
The lot has no primary building;
The site fronts onto 3 streets; and
Educational facilities that exceed 1,000 m2 of gross floor area.
Driveways are permitted when they extend into the Side or Rear Yard of the property, or when used to access an Attached Garage.
Shared driveways and Curb Cuts are permitted with adjacent properties, subject to a Right-of-way agreement between property owners.
The amenity requirements in Section 3.13 shall be apply.
The main lobby of any multi-unit dwelling shall exit onto an adjoining street, connected by a minimum 2 m wide hard surfaced walkway to the adjacent sidewalk.
Grade-related dwelling units, where permitted, shall include a patio door, and a landscaped private patio no less than 6 m2.
The minimum required ground floor height shall be 3.5 m.
A 4m (min) wide public boardwalk shall be reserved along all properties that border the Hillsborough River or Charlottetown Harbour in any zone of the 500 lot area except the Port Zone (P).
The boardwalk shall be built to match the existing standard of a wooden or hard- surfaced unit-paver in coordination with the City and/or Charlottetown Area Development Corporation.

review_flags.description: PDF text order was extracted with pypdf; verify section order, column flow, and table layout before normalization.
Some extracted text was not safely assigned to a labeled section and is preserved in content_blocks.
The source section contains table, figure, or schedule text. Values are preserved as extracted text and require source PDF layout review before normalization.
